title: Rhythm: news headline practice
description: Mixed-case prose with proper punctuation — like reading headlines

tips.text: No new keys. We work the rhythm on plain words, the kind a headline is built from, before the capitals and figures arrive.
Keep your hands home and your eyes on the screen. Type each word at one even pace, the same throughout. Regularity carries a long line further than speed.

tips.title: A measured pace
tips.text: Keep the same quiet beat between keys and hold it steady. Even and slow outlasts quick and uneven. Even keystrokes read like clean copy.

tips.title: Rhythm: launches
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: launches launches launches. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: starship
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: starship starship starship. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: planet
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: planet planet planet. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.text: A clean headline reads as if it cost no effort. That ease comes from an even hand, built here, on plain words, one steady keystroke at a time.
Worked through every step? Open the trainer below and make one full run on the lesson text. That run saves your progress and unlocks the next lesson.

character_tips.anna: News headline style — practice for content writers and journalists.
character_tips.maxim: Title case + numbers + punctuation = professional writing flow.
character_tips.klavochka: Like writing the front page — exciting! 📰✨
